concerns.text: T021 (MSE implementation) and T022 (Aggregation) are marked [P] (parallel-safe) but depend on the output of T017 (US1: `data/processed/subject_timeseries.csv`). The task list places US2 tasks in a separate phase but does not explicitly enforce the data-flow dependency that US1 must complete before US2 begins. While phases imply order, the [P] tag on T021/T022 is semantically incorrect if they can run before T017 finishes. The 'Dependencies & Execution Order' section correctly notes US2 depends on US1, but the task-level [P] tags create a contradiction.

T029 (Robust Regression) and T030 (FDR Correction) are marked [P] but depend on the output of T025 (US2: `data/processed/entropy_metrics.csv`). Similar to the US1->US2 issue, the [P] tag implies these can run in parallel with other tasks, but they strictly require the artifact produced by US2. The 'Dependencies' section notes US3 depends on US2, but the task-level parallel tags are misleading and violate the 'Producer before consumer' rule.

T015 and T016 are NOT marked [P] while T013 and T014 are. T015 (motion_check) and T016 (cleaning) logically depend on the output of T013 (download) and T014 (parcellate). The lack of [P] on T015/16 is correct, but the description for T016 ('handle missing NIH Toolbox scores') implies it might need data from T013 (phenotypes) and T014 (time series). Ensure the task description clarifies that T016 consumes the output of T013/T014, not just that it 'handles' missing data.

T035 (Benchmark) and T036 (CI Run) are placed in Phase 6. T036 explicitly requires running the 'full pipeline'. This implies a dependency on the completion of T033 (Modeling) and T034 (Completeness Report). While Phase 6 is after Phase 5, the [P] tag on T035 suggests it can run in parallel with T036 or other Phase 6 tasks. T035 (benchmark script creation) is independent, but T036 (execution) is a consumer of the entire pipeline. Ensure T036 is not marked [P] if it implies running the full pipeline which depends on all prior phases.
